fault_hypothesis: Lines 72-73 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams share the same last value, but this deduplication logic is wrong — the merged result should preserve all elements including duplicates from both streams.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid merged streams — this spurious deduplication always wrongly removes an element.
After initialization, merged must be empty.
After the pop branch executes, merged should still have at least len(left)+len(right)-1 elements, but the bug causes it to drop one too many.
The merged result must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements since all elements from both streams must be preserved.
